text:
Derek Chisorahas waded into the row surrounding Algerian boxer Imane Khelif being allowed to compete in the women's category at theOlympics.Imane is one of two female boxers to have been cleared by the International Olympic Committeedespite being deemed biologically male after a DNA test.Khelif was found to have XY chromosomes by the International Boxing Association, who conducted DNA testing at last year's Women's World Boxing Championships. She was ejected from the tournament but will be allowed to box at the Olympics, where the IBA does not have jurisdiction.Chisora offered his thoughts on the topic with a post onX, formerly Twitter, ahead of Khelif's opening bout in the welterweight division later this week. The 40-year-old, who defeated Joe Joyce on points last weekend, shared a video of Khelif beating up an opponent alongside the caption: "Is this fair?"

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Clickhereto buy.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Former world champion Barry McGuigan has also taken to social media to suggest that Khelif should not be allowed to box female opponents at the Olympics. He wrote: "It’s shocking that they were actually allowed to get this far. What is going on?"Brianda Tamara, a Mexican boxer who fought Khelif at last year's World Championships, said that her punching power was more than she had ever felt against other female opponents."When I fought with her I felt very out of my depth," said Tamara on social media. "Her blows hurt me a lot. I don’t think I had ever felt like that in my 13 years as a boxer, nor in my sparring with men. Thank God that day I got out of the ring safely, and it’s good that they finally realised."

Don't miss:Jack Draper blasts Olympics after being made to drink warm water in searing heat

The IOC have already insisted that all boxers at the Olympics 'comply with the competition’s eligibility and entry regulations, as well as all applicable medical regulations'.Mark Adams, a spokesperson for the governing body, said on Tuesday: "These athletes have competed many times before for many years. They haven't just suddenly arrived. They competed in Tokyo. The federation needs to make the rules to make sure that there is fairness."But, at the same time, there is the ability for everyone to take part that wants to. That is a difficult balance. In the end, the experts for each sport are the people who work in that. If there is a big advantage, that clearly is not acceptable, but that needs to be a decision made at that level."

title: Derek Chisora wades in on Imane Khelif Olympics row as boxer cleared to fight | Boxing | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Archie Griggs
description: Imane Khelif has been cleared to fight in the women's boxing at the Olympics.
